title: Gobl Schema
description: A Gobl schema is an object where each key is a table name. Pass it to $db->loadSchema() or $db->ns()->schema(). Table names must match: ^[a-z]([a-z0-9_]*[a-z0-9])?$ - lowercase letters, digits, underscores; starts and ends with a letter or digit. Example: { "users": { "columns": { "id": { "type": "bigint", "auto_increment": true } } } }

propertyNames.description: Table name - lowercase letters, digits, underscores; starts and ends with a letter or digit. (Table::NAME_PATTERN)

table.description: A single table definition. Only 'columns' is required; all other keys are optional. Example: { "plural_name": "users", "singular_name": "user", "column_prefix": "user", "columns": { "id": { "type": "bigint", "auto_increment": true, "unsigned": true } }, "constraints": [{ "type": "primary_key", "columns": ["id"] }] }

plural_name.description: Plural name used for ORM class naming (e.g. 'users' -> generates 'UsersController', 'UsersQuery', 'UsersResults', 'UsersCrud'). Must match Table::NAME_PATTERN: lowercase letters, digits, underscores; starts/ends with letter or digit.

singular_name.description: Singular name used for ORM entity class naming (e.g. 'user' -> generates 'User' entity class). Must match Table::NAME_PATTERN.

column_prefix.description: Prefix prepended to every column's SQL name (e.g. 'user' + column 'id' = SQL column 'user_id'). Must match Column::PREFIX_PATTERN: lowercase letters, digits, underscores.

prefix.description: Override the database-level table prefix for this table only (overrides DbConfig::db_table_prefix). Must match Table::PREFIX_PATTERN: letters, digits, underscores (mixed case allowed).

namespace.description: PHP namespace for the generated ORM classes. Overrides the namespace passed to $db->ns() for this specific table.

diff_key.description: Stable identifier used by the Diff engine when comparing two schema versions. Defaults to the table name. Set this to preserve diff continuity when renaming a table.

morph_type.description: Identifier used for polymorphic relations. When a morph link targets this table, the child row's 'type' column is set to this value. Defaults to the table name. Must match Table::NAME_PATTERN.

charset.description: Character set override for this table (MySQL e.g. 'utf8mb4').

collate.description: Collation override for this table (MySQL e.g. 'utf8mb4_unicode_ci').

private.description: When true, all columns in this table are treated as private by default (equivalent to setting 'private: true' on every column).

meta.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata attached to the table. Available inside Blate templates and code generators. Any JSON value is accepted.

columns.description: Map of column short name -> column definition. The short name (without the column_prefix) must match Column::NAME_PATTERN: ^[a-z]([a-z0-9_]*[a-z0-9])?$. The full SQL column name is column_prefix + '_' + short_name.

propertyNames.description: Column short name - lowercase letters, digits, underscores; starts and ends with a letter or digit.

constraints.description: List of table-level constraints: primary_key, unique_key, foreign_key. Processed after all tables are loaded so cross-table FK references can be resolved.

relations.description: Map of relation name -> relation definition. Relations are processed last (after constraints). For simple FK-based relations, just set 'type' and 'target' - the link columns are auto-detected from the foreign key constraint.

propertyNames.description: Relation name - letters, digits, underscores, hyphens; starts and ends with letter or digit. Must not be a reserved ORM name (save, new, table, crud, qb, ctrl, results, etc.).

indexes.description: List of indexes for this table. Distinct from constraints - indexes improve query performance but do not enforce uniqueness (unless combined with a unique_key constraint).

column.description: A column definition. Either a shorthand reference string ('ref:table.col' or 'cp:table.col') or a full options object. The allowed properties depend on the 'type' value.

oneOf.description: 'ref:table.column' inherits the target column's type options at schema-loading time. auto_increment is stripped - use this for foreign key columns. 'cp:table.column' also inherits type options but preserves auto_increment. Both create independent columns; extra options can be merged using the array form: { "type": "ref:users.id", "nullable": true }. diff_key is always stripped from both.

Custom column type registered via TypeUtils::addTypeProvider(). The 'type' value must match a custom type name registered at runtime. All properties are allowed since the schema cannot know the custom type's option set.

type.description: Custom type name (not one of the built-in types). Must be registered via TypeUtils::addTypeProvider() before schema loading.

columnBigint.description: 64-bit signed integer column (BIGINT). Typical use: primary keys ('auto_increment: true, unsigned: true'), large counters, foreign keys. Stored as BIGINT in MySQL/PostgreSQL and INTEGER in SQLite.

unsigned.description: Disallow negative values. Accepted range becomes 0 to +max.

auto_increment.description: Auto-increment the value on INSERT. Typically used on the primary key column. Only one column per table may use auto_increment.

min.description: Minimum allowed value (inclusive). Pass as a string to preserve 64-bit precision (e.g. '9007199254740993').

max.description: Maximum allowed value (inclusive). Pass as a string for 64-bit precision.

nullable.description: Whether the column accepts NULL. When false (default), NULL values are rejected during validation. Alias: 'null'.

default.description: Default value applied when no value is provided on INSERT. For bool use 1/0. For date use a Unix timestamp integer or ISO 8601 string.

prefix.description: Override the table-level column_prefix for this specific column only. Must match Column::PREFIX_PATTERN.

diff_key.description: Stable identifier used by the Diff engine when comparing two schema versions. Defaults to the column name. Set to preserve diff continuity when renaming a column.

private.description: Mark the column as private. Excluded from ORM outputs by default and triggers BeforePrivateColumnWrite CRUD events on write.

sensitive.description: Mark the column as sensitive (e.g. passwords, tokens). Triggers BeforeSensitiveColumnWrite CRUD events. Real value replaced by sensitive_redacted_value in logs.

sensitive_redacted_value.description: Replacement value shown in logs/serialization when redacting this sensitive column. Defaults to null.

meta.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata for Blate templates and code generators. Any JSON value is accepted.

validator:pre.description: FQCN of a TypeValidatorInterface implementation to run BEFORE built-in type validation. Useful for input normalization.

validator:post.description: FQCN of a TypeValidatorInterface implementation to run AFTER built-in type validation. Useful for cross-field business rules.

columnInt.description: 32-bit signed integer column (INT). Use bigint for primary keys that might exceed 2^31. Stored as INT in MySQL/PostgreSQL and INTEGER in SQLite.

unsigned.description: Disallow negative values. Accepted range becomes 0 to +max.

auto_increment.description: Auto-increment the value on INSERT. Typically used on the primary key column. Only one column per table may use auto_increment.

nullable.description: Whether the column accepts NULL. When false (default), NULL values are rejected during validation. Alias: 'null'.

default.description: Default value applied when no value is provided on INSERT. For bool use 1/0. For date use a Unix timestamp integer or ISO 8601 string.

prefix.description: Override the table-level column_prefix for this specific column only. Must match Column::PREFIX_PATTERN.

diff_key.description: Stable identifier used by the Diff engine when comparing two schema versions. Defaults to the column name. Set to preserve diff continuity when renaming a column.

private.description: Mark the column as private. Excluded from ORM outputs by default and triggers BeforePrivateColumnWrite CRUD events on write.

sensitive.description: Mark the column as sensitive (e.g. passwords, tokens). Triggers BeforeSensitiveColumnWrite CRUD events. Real value replaced by sensitive_redacted_value in logs.

sensitive_redacted_value.description: Replacement value shown in logs/serialization when redacting this sensitive column. Defaults to null.

meta.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata for Blate templates and code generators. Any JSON value is accepted.

validator:post.description: FQCN of a TypeValidatorInterface implementation to run AFTER built-in type validation. Useful for cross-field business rules.

columnFloat.description: IEEE 754 double-precision floating-point column (DOUBLE / FLOAT8). For monetary amounts use 'decimal' instead. Stored as DOUBLE in MySQL and FLOAT8 in PostgreSQL/SQLite.

unsigned.description: Disallow negative values. Accepted range becomes 0 to +max.

mantissa.description: Number of decimal places for SQL schema generation hint (FLOAT(n)). Does not affect PHP validation.

nullable.description: Whether the column accepts NULL. When false (default), NULL values are rejected during validation. Alias: 'null'.

default.description: Default value applied when no value is provided on INSERT. For bool use 1/0. For date use a Unix timestamp integer or ISO 8601 string.

prefix.description: Override the table-level column_prefix for this specific column only. Must match Column::PREFIX_PATTERN.

diff_key.description: Stable identifier used by the Diff engine when comparing two schema versions. Defaults to the column name. Set to preserve diff continuity when renaming a column.

sensitive.description: Mark the column as sensitive (e.g. passwords, tokens). Triggers BeforeSensitiveColumnWrite CRUD events. Real value replaced by sensitive_redacted_value in logs.

sensitive_redacted_value.description: Replacement value shown in logs/serialization when redacting this sensitive column. Defaults to null.

validator:post.description: FQCN of a TypeValidatorInterface implementation to run AFTER built-in type validation. Useful for cross-field business rules.

columnDecimal.description: Fixed-point decimal column (DECIMAL(p, s)). Use for monetary amounts and other values requiring exact decimal representation. Stored as DECIMAL(p,s) in MySQL/PostgreSQL and NUMERIC(p,s) in SQLite.

unsigned.description: Disallow negative values. Accepted range becomes 0 to +max.

min.description: Minimum allowed value (inclusive). Pass as a string to preserve 64-bit precision (e.g. '9007199254740993').

max.description: Maximum allowed value (inclusive). Pass as a string for 64-bit precision.

precision.description: Total number of significant digits (e.g. 10 for DECIMAL(10,2)). Required with 'scale'.

scale.description: Number of digits after the decimal point (must be <= precision). Example: precision=10, scale=2 gives DECIMAL(10,2).

nullable.description: Whether the column accepts NULL. When false (default), NULL values are rejected during validation. Alias: 'null'.

default.description: Default value applied when no value is provided on INSERT. For bool use 1/0. For date use a Unix timestamp integer or ISO 8601 string.

prefix.description: Override the table-level column_prefix for this specific column only. Must match Column::PREFIX_PATTERN.

sensitive.description: Mark the column as sensitive (e.g. passwords, tokens). Triggers BeforeSensitiveColumnWrite CRUD events. Real value replaced by sensitive_redacted_value in logs.

sensitive_redacted_value.description: Replacement value shown in logs/serialization when redacting this sensitive column. Defaults to null.

validator:post.description: FQCN of a TypeValidatorInterface implementation to run AFTER built-in type validation. Useful for cross-field business rules.

columnBool.description: Boolean column stored as TINYINT(1) (MySQL), BOOLEAN (PostgreSQL), or INTEGER (SQLite). PHP true/false are stored as 1/0.

strict.description: When true (default), only true/false/1/0 are accepted. When false, also accepts 'yes'/'no', 'on'/'off', 'y'/'n', etc.

nullable.description: Whether the column accepts NULL. When false (default), NULL values are rejected during validation. Alias: 'null'.

default.description: Default value applied when no value is provided on INSERT. For bool use 1/0. For date use a Unix timestamp integer or ISO 8601 string.

prefix.description: Override the table-level column_prefix for this specific column only. Must match Column::PREFIX_PATTERN.

diff_key.description: Stable identifier used by the Diff engine when comparing two schema versions. Defaults to the column name. Set to preserve diff continuity when renaming a column.

private.description: Mark the column as private. Excluded from ORM outputs by default and triggers BeforePrivateColumnWrite CRUD events on write.

sensitive.description: Mark the column as sensitive (e.g. passwords, tokens). Triggers BeforeSensitiveColumnWrite CRUD events. Real value replaced by sensitive_redacted_value in logs.

sensitive_redacted_value.description: Replacement value shown in logs/serialization when redacting this sensitive column. Defaults to null.

meta.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata for Blate templates and code generators. Any JSON value is accepted.

validator:pre.description: FQCN of a TypeValidatorInterface implementation to run BEFORE built-in type validation. Useful for input normalization.

validator:post.description: FQCN of a TypeValidatorInterface implementation to run AFTER built-in type validation. Useful for cross-field business rules.

columnString.description: Variable-length string column (VARCHAR/TEXT). Use 'max' to set the length. Add 'medium: true' or 'long: true' for larger content. Stored as VARCHAR(max) in MySQL (TEXT when max > 255 or unset) and TEXT in PostgreSQL/SQLite.

max.description: Maximum character length (inclusive). Maps to VARCHAR(max) in MySQL. Default: unlimited (TEXT).

pattern.description: PHP PCRE regex pattern the value must fully match (include delimiters, e.g. '/^[a-z]+$/i'). Validation uses preg_match().

one_of.description: Restrict the value to one of these exact strings. Useful for small enum-like string columns without a PHP BackedEnum class.

truncate.description: Silently truncate the value to 'max' characters instead of raising a validation error.

multiline.description: Allow newline characters (\n, \r) in the value. When false (default), newlines cause a validation error.

trim.description: Trim leading/trailing whitespace before validation and storage.

medium.description: Hint to use MEDIUMTEXT storage in MySQL (up to ~16 MB). Has no effect on PostgreSQL/SQLite or on PHP validation.

long.description: Hint to use LONGTEXT storage in MySQL (up to ~4 GB). Has no effect on PostgreSQL/SQLite or on PHP validation.

nullable.description: Whether the column accepts NULL. When false (default), NULL values are rejected during validation. Alias: 'null'.

default.description: Default value applied when no value is provided on INSERT. For bool use 1/0. For date use a Unix timestamp integer or ISO 8601 string.

prefix.description: Override the table-level column_prefix for this specific column only. Must match Column::PREFIX_PATTERN.

diff_key.description: Stable identifier used by the Diff engine when comparing two schema versions. Defaults to the column name. Set to preserve diff continuity when renaming a column.

sensitive.description: Mark the column as sensitive (e.g. passwords, tokens). Triggers BeforeSensitiveColumnWrite CRUD events. Real value replaced by sensitive_redacted_value in logs.

sensitive_redacted_value.description: Replacement value shown in logs/serialization when redacting this sensitive column. Defaults to null.

validator:post.description: FQCN of a TypeValidatorInterface implementation to run AFTER built-in type validation. Useful for cross-field business rules.

columnEnum.description: Enum column backed by a PHP BackedEnum (string or int backing). The column stores the enum's backing value. On read, the stored value is passed to YourEnum::from() to return a typed enum instance. Required: 'enum_class'.

enum_class.description: FQCN of a PHP BackedEnum subclass. The enum cases define the allowed values. Example: 'App\\Enums\\UserStatus'.

nullable.description: Whether the column accepts NULL. When false (default), NULL values are rejected during validation. Alias: 'null'.

default.description: Default value applied when no value is provided on INSERT. For bool use 1/0. For date use a Unix timestamp integer or ISO 8601 string.

prefix.description: Override the table-level column_prefix for this specific column only. Must match Column::PREFIX_PATTERN.

diff_key.description: Stable identifier used by the Diff engine when comparing two schema versions. Defaults to the column name. Set to preserve diff continuity when renaming a column.

private.description: Mark the column as private. Excluded from ORM outputs by default and triggers BeforePrivateColumnWrite CRUD events on write.

sensitive.description: Mark the column as sensitive (e.g. passwords, tokens). Triggers BeforeSensitiveColumnWrite CRUD events. Real value replaced by sensitive_redacted_value in logs.

sensitive_redacted_value.description: Replacement value shown in logs/serialization when redacting this sensitive column. Defaults to null.

meta.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata for Blate templates and code generators. Any JSON value is accepted.

validator:pre.description: FQCN of a TypeValidatorInterface implementation to run BEFORE built-in type validation. Useful for input normalization.

validator:post.description: FQCN of a TypeValidatorInterface implementation to run AFTER built-in type validation. Useful for cross-field business rules.

columnDate.description: Date/time column stored as BIGINT (Unix timestamp seconds) or DECIMAL(20,6) (microseconds). Input and output use PHP date() format. Automatic populate on INSERT/UPDATE is supported via 'auto: true'.

auto.description: Automatically set to the current timestamp on INSERT. Columns named 'updated_at' are also updated on every UPDATE.

format.description: PHP date() format for input/output conversion. Use 'timestamp' (default, Unix integer seconds) or any PHP date format (e.g. 'Y-m-d H:i:s', 'Y-m-d', DATE_ATOM).

precision.description: Set to 'microseconds' to store sub-second precision as DECIMAL(20,6) instead of BIGINT. Enables storing fractional seconds.

min.description: Minimum allowed date/time value (inclusive). Accepts a Unix timestamp integer or a date string in the column's format.

max.description: Maximum allowed date/time value (inclusive). Accepts a Unix timestamp integer or a date string in the column's format.

nullable.description: Whether the column accepts NULL. When false (default), NULL values are rejected during validation. Alias: 'null'.

default.description: Default value applied when no value is provided on INSERT. For bool use 1/0. For date use a Unix timestamp integer or ISO 8601 string.

prefix.description: Override the table-level column_prefix for this specific column only. Must match Column::PREFIX_PATTERN.

diff_key.description: Stable identifier used by the Diff engine when comparing two schema versions. Defaults to the column name. Set to preserve diff continuity when renaming a column.

private.description: Mark the column as private. Excluded from ORM outputs by default and triggers BeforePrivateColumnWrite CRUD events on write.

sensitive.description: Mark the column as sensitive (e.g. passwords, tokens). Triggers BeforeSensitiveColumnWrite CRUD events. Real value replaced by sensitive_redacted_value in logs.

sensitive_redacted_value.description: Replacement value shown in logs/serialization when redacting this sensitive column. Defaults to null.

meta.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata for Blate templates and code generators. Any JSON value is accepted.

validator:pre.description: FQCN of a TypeValidatorInterface implementation to run BEFORE built-in type validation. Useful for input normalization.

validator:post.description: FQCN of a TypeValidatorInterface implementation to run AFTER built-in type validation. Useful for cross-field business rules.

columnMap.description: JSON object column (key-value map). Stored as TEXT or native JSON. Use 'native_json: true' to enable JSON path operators (contains, has_key) in filters. Maps to PHP associative arrays on read.

native_json.description: Use the database's native JSON column type (MySQL JSON, PostgreSQL JSONB) instead of TEXT. Enables JSON path operators (contains, has_key) in filters.

big.description: When native_json is false, use MEDIUMTEXT (MySQL) or TEXT (PostgreSQL/SQLite) for larger JSON documents.

nullable.description: Whether the column accepts NULL. When false (default), NULL values are rejected during validation. Alias: 'null'.

default.description: Default value applied when no value is provided on INSERT. For bool use 1/0. For date use a Unix timestamp integer or ISO 8601 string.

prefix.description: Override the table-level column_prefix for this specific column only. Must match Column::PREFIX_PATTERN.

diff_key.description: Stable identifier used by the Diff engine when comparing two schema versions. Defaults to the column name. Set to preserve diff continuity when renaming a column.

private.description: Mark the column as private. Excluded from ORM outputs by default and triggers BeforePrivateColumnWrite CRUD events on write.

sensitive.description: Mark the column as sensitive (e.g. passwords, tokens). Triggers BeforeSensitiveColumnWrite CRUD events. Real value replaced by sensitive_redacted_value in logs.

sensitive_redacted_value.description: Replacement value shown in logs/serialization when redacting this sensitive column. Defaults to null.

meta.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata for Blate templates and code generators. Any JSON value is accepted.

validator:pre.description: FQCN of a TypeValidatorInterface implementation to run BEFORE built-in type validation. Useful for input normalization.

validator:post.description: FQCN of a TypeValidatorInterface implementation to run AFTER built-in type validation. Useful for cross-field business rules.

columnList.description: JSON array column (ordered list). Stored as TEXT or native JSON. Use 'native_json: true' to enable JSON path operators in filters. Use 'list_of' for typed code generation.

native_json.description: Use the database's native JSON column type (MySQL JSON, PostgreSQL JSONB) instead of TEXT. Enables JSON path operators (contains, has_key) in filters.

big.description: When native_json is false, use MEDIUMTEXT (MySQL) or TEXT (PostgreSQL/SQLite) for larger JSON documents.

list_of.description: Element type hint for code generation and optional runtime revival. Use an ORMUniversalType value ('string', 'int', 'bigint', 'float', 'bool') for metadata only, or a FQCN implementing JsonOfInterface for typed revival (each element is passed through ClassName::revive() on read).

nullable.description: Whether the column accepts NULL. When false (default), NULL values are rejected during validation. Alias: 'null'.

default.description: Default value applied when no value is provided on INSERT. For bool use 1/0. For date use a Unix timestamp integer or ISO 8601 string.

prefix.description: Override the table-level column_prefix for this specific column only. Must match Column::PREFIX_PATTERN.

diff_key.description: Stable identifier used by the Diff engine when comparing two schema versions. Defaults to the column name. Set to preserve diff continuity when renaming a column.

private.description: Mark the column as private. Excluded from ORM outputs by default and triggers BeforePrivateColumnWrite CRUD events on write.

sensitive.description: Mark the column as sensitive (e.g. passwords, tokens). Triggers BeforeSensitiveColumnWrite CRUD events. Real value replaced by sensitive_redacted_value in logs.

sensitive_redacted_value.description: Replacement value shown in logs/serialization when redacting this sensitive column. Defaults to null.

meta.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata for Blate templates and code generators. Any JSON value is accepted.

validator:pre.description: FQCN of a TypeValidatorInterface implementation to run BEFORE built-in type validation. Useful for input normalization.

validator:post.description: FQCN of a TypeValidatorInterface implementation to run AFTER built-in type validation. Useful for cross-field business rules.

columnJson.description: Raw JSON document column (object or array root). Stored as TEXT or native JSON. Use 'native_json: true' for JSON path operators. Use 'json_of' for typed PHP objects. Use 'json_data_type' to restrict to array or object root.

native_json.description: Use the database's native JSON column type (MySQL JSON, PostgreSQL JSONB) instead of TEXT. Enables JSON path operators (contains, has_key) in filters.

big.description: When native_json is false, use MEDIUMTEXT (MySQL) or TEXT (PostgreSQL/SQLite) for larger JSON documents.

json_of.description: FQCN of a class implementing JsonOfInterface. On read, stored JSON is decoded and passed to ClassName::revive(). On write, instances are JSON-encoded. Enables typed ORM getters.

json_data_type.description: Restricts the kind of JSON root document. 'any' (default): object or array. 'array': only a JSON array. 'object': only a JSON object. Ignored when json_of is set.

nullable.description: Whether the column accepts NULL. When false (default), NULL values are rejected during validation. Alias: 'null'.

default.description: Default value applied when no value is provided on INSERT. For bool use 1/0. For date use a Unix timestamp integer or ISO 8601 string.

prefix.description: Override the table-level column_prefix for this specific column only. Must match Column::PREFIX_PATTERN.

diff_key.description: Stable identifier used by the Diff engine when comparing two schema versions. Defaults to the column name. Set to preserve diff continuity when renaming a column.

private.description: Mark the column as private. Excluded from ORM outputs by default and triggers BeforePrivateColumnWrite CRUD events on write.

sensitive.description: Mark the column as sensitive (e.g. passwords, tokens). Triggers BeforeSensitiveColumnWrite CRUD events. Real value replaced by sensitive_redacted_value in logs.

sensitive_redacted_value.description: Replacement value shown in logs/serialization when redacting this sensitive column. Defaults to null.

meta.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata for Blate templates and code generators. Any JSON value is accepted.

validator:pre.description: FQCN of a TypeValidatorInterface implementation to run BEFORE built-in type validation. Useful for input normalization.

validator:post.description: FQCN of a TypeValidatorInterface implementation to run AFTER built-in type validation. Useful for cross-field business rules.

constraint.description: A table-level constraint. The 'type' and 'columns' properties are always required. For 'foreign_key', 'reference' is also required.

type.description: primary_key: designates the PK column(s). Only one primary_key per table. unique_key: enforces uniqueness across the listed columns. foreign_key: adds a FK constraint referencing another table's PK or UK.

columns.description: primary_key / unique_key: array of column short names. foreign_key: object mapping { localColumn: referencedColumn }. Column names are short names (without the column_prefix).

oneOf.description: Array of column short names (primary_key / unique_key).
{ localColumn: referencedColumn } mapping (foreign_key). Both keys and values are short column names.

reference.description: foreign_key: name of the referenced table (must already be defined in the schema). Must match Table::NAME_PATTERN.

update.description: foreign_key: ON UPDATE referential action. 'cascade': propagate updates. 'set_null': set FK to NULL. 'set_default': set FK to its DEFAULT. 'restrict': reject the parent update. 'none': no action (database default).

delete.description: foreign_key: ON DELETE referential action. 'cascade': delete child rows. 'set_null': set FK to NULL. 'set_default': set FK to its DEFAULT. 'restrict': reject the parent delete. 'none': no action (database default).

name.description: foreign_key: optional explicit constraint name (max 64 chars). Auto-generated from table/column names when omitted.

relation.description: A named relation from this table to a target table. For simple FK-based relations (one-to-many, many-to-one), omit 'link' - Gobl auto-detects the join columns from the foreign key constraint. Use 'link' for polymorphic (morph), pivot (through), or custom (join) cases.

type.description: Cardinality of the relation. one-to-one: single result from target. one-to-many: list from target (host is parent). many-to-one: single result from target (host has FK). many-to-many: list via pivot table (requires link.type='through').

target.description: Short name of the target table. Must be defined in the same schema. Must match Table::NAME_PATTERN.

link.description: Explicit link configuration. Omit to auto-detect from foreign key constraints. Required for morph, through, and join link types.

columns.description: Deprecated: use 'link: { type: "columns", columns: {...} }' instead. Direct column mapping: { localColumn: targetColumn }.

relationLink.description: Describes how the host table and target table are joined. The 'type' property selects the strategy; other properties depend on the type.

type.description: 'columns': link via explicit or auto-detected FK columns (default for most relations). 'morph': polymorphic link via {prefix}_id / {prefix}_type columns. 'through': many-to-many via a pivot table. 'join': multi-step custom join across intermediate tables.

columns.description: columns link: explicit { localColumn: targetColumn } mapping. Omit to auto-detect from FK constraints. Example: { "user_id": "id" } links this table's 'user_id' column to the target's 'id' column.

filters.description: Extra filter conditions applied to the join query, in addition to the key column constraints. Accepts the same filter formats as Filters::fromArray().

prefix.description: morph link: naming prefix for the morph columns. If 'prefix' is 'taggable', the child table is expected to have columns 'taggable_id' and 'taggable_type'. Required unless both 'child_key_column' and 'child_type_column' are provided.

parent_type.description: morph link: value stored in the child's type column to identify the parent table. Defaults to the parent table's morph_type (which defaults to the table name).

parent_key_column.description: morph link: short name of the column in the parent table used as the key (usually the primary key). Auto-detected from the parent's primary key constraint when omitted.

child_key_column.description: morph link: short name of the column in the child table that holds the parent's key value (e.g. 'taggable_id'). Required when 'prefix' is not set.

child_type_column.description: morph link: short name of the column in the child table that holds the parent's type string (e.g. 'taggable_type'). Required when 'prefix' is not set.

pivot_table.description: through link: name of the pivot/join table. Must be defined in the same schema. The pivot table must have FK columns to both the host and target tables.

host_to_pivot.description: through link: optional explicit link configuration from the host table to the pivot table. Omit to auto-detect from FK constraints.

type.description: Link type for the host-to-pivot leg.

pivot_to_target.description: through link: optional explicit link configuration from the pivot table to the target table. Omit to auto-detect from FK constraints.

type.description: Link type for the pivot-to-target leg.

steps.description: join link: ordered list of join steps. Each step joins to an intermediate table. The final step lands on the target table (auto-linked if omitted from steps).

join.description: Name of the table to join to in this step.

link.description: Link configuration for this step (type + columns). Defaults to 'columns' with auto-detection.

index.description: A table index definition. Indexes speed up queries but do not enforce uniqueness (use a unique_key constraint for that). Supported index types vary by driver.

columns.description: Short column names to include in the index. Order matters for composite B-Tree indexes. Each item must match Column::NAME_PATTERN.

type.description: Index type. Defaults to BTREE (supported by all drivers). HASH: equality-only (MySQL MEMORY/NDB, PostgreSQL). MYSQL_FULLTEXT: full-text search (MySQL/MariaDB). MYSQL_SPATIAL: geometric data (MySQL). PGSQL_GIN: arrays, JSONB, tsvector (PostgreSQL). PGSQL_GIST: geometry, ranges (PostgreSQL). PGSQL_BRIN: large append-only tables (PostgreSQL). PGSQL_SPGIST: space-partitioned data (PostgreSQL).
